subject: Physics
q: A particle is rotating in a circular path and at any instant its motion can be described as $\theta = \dfrac{5t^4}{40} - \dfrac{t^3}{3}$. The angular acceleration of the particle after $10$ seconds is _______ rad/s$^2$.
question: A particle is rotating in a circular path and at any instant its motion can be described as $\theta = \dfrac{5t^4}{40} - \dfrac{t^3}{3}$. The angular acceleration of the particle after $10$ seconds is _______ rad/s$^2$.
solution:
Given $\theta = \dfrac{5t^4}{40} - \dfrac{t^3}{3} = \dfrac{t^4}{8} - \dfrac{t^3}{3}$

Angular velocity $\omega = \dfrac{d\theta}{dt} = \dfrac{4t^3}{8} - \dfrac{3t^2}{3} = \dfrac{t^3}{2} - t^2$

Angular acceleration $\alpha = \dfrac{d\omega}{dt} = \dfrac{3t^2}{2} - 2t$

At $t = 10$ s, $\alpha = \dfrac{3(10)^2}{2} - 2(10)$

$\alpha = 150 - 20 = 130$ rad/s$^2$

Answer: $130$

subject: Physics
q: Consider a disc of mass 5 kg, radius 2 m, rotating with angular velocity of 10 rad s-1 about an axis perpendicular to the plane of rotation. An identical disc is kept gently over the rotating disc along the same axis. The energy dissipated so that both the discs continue to rotate together without slipping is _________J.
question: Consider a disc of mass 5 kg, radius 2 m, rotating with angular velocity of 10 rad s-1 about an axis perpendicular to the plane of rotation. An identical disc is kept gently over the rotating disc along the same axis. The energy dissipated so that both the discs continue to rotate together without slipping is _________J.
solution:
Initial angular momentum, Li=Iωi=MR22·ω=100 kg m2 s-1

Initial kinetic energy, Ei=12·MR22·ω2=500 J

From conservation of angular momentum, we can write

L→i=L→f⇒100=2Iωf

⇒ωf=5 rad s-1

Ef=2×12·5(2)22·(5)2=250 J

Therefore, required energy loss ΔE=250 J

subject: Physics
q: A uniform bar of length 12 cm and mass $20 m$ lies on a smooth horizontal table. Two point masses $m$ and $2 m$ are moving in opposite directions with same speed of $v$ and in the same plane as the bar, as shown in figure. These masses strike the bar simultaneously and get stuck to it. After collision the entire system is rotating with angular frequency $\omega$. The ratio of $v$ and $\omega$ is :
question: A uniform bar of length 12 cm and mass $20 m$ lies on a smooth horizontal table. Two point masses $m$ and $2 m$ are moving in opposite directions with same speed of $v$ and in the same plane as the bar, as shown in figure. These masses strike the bar simultaneously and get stuck to it. After collision the entire system is rotating with angular frequency $\omega$. The ratio of $v$ and $\omega$ is :
solution:
Linear momentum conservation: $mv - 2mv = 23m \cdot v_{cm}$, giving $v_{cm} = -\frac{v}{23}$.
Angular momentum about center of mass from the collision geometry:
Taking collision points 2 cm and 4 cm from the 12 cm bar's center at different positions, the angular momentum is $L = m \cdot v \cdot r_1 + (-2m) \cdot v \cdot r_2$.
Calculating moment of inertia: $I = \frac{1}{12}(20m)(0.12)^2 + m(0.02)^2 + 2m(0.04)^2 = 0.0276m$
The angular frequency $\omega$ is obtained from $\omega = \frac{L}{I}$, giving ratio $\frac{v}{\omega} = 33$.

Torque about 'Q'

τ→Q=r→2×F→=15-203k^

q: Consider a uniform square plate of side ' $a$ ' and mass ' $m$ '. The moment of inertia of this plate about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through one of its corners is
question: Consider a uniform square plate of side ' $a$ ' and mass ' $m$ '. The moment of inertia of this plate about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through one of its corners is
solution:
$$
I=I_{c m}+m\left(\frac{a \sqrt{2}}{2}\right)^2=\frac{m a^2}{6}+\frac{m a^2}{2}=\frac{2}{3} m a^2
$$

Consider a badminton racket with length scales as shown in the figure.

If the mass of the linear and circular portions of the badminton racket are same (M) and the mass of the threads are negligible, the moment of inertia of the racket about an axis perpendicular to the handle and in the plane of the ring at, r2 distance from the end A of the handle will be ______Mr2.
question:
Consider a badminton racket with length scales as shown in the figure.

If the mass of the linear and circular portions of the badminton racket are same (M) and the mass of the threads are negligible, the moment of inertia of the racket about an axis perpendicular to the handle and in the plane of the ring at, r2 distance from the end A of the handle will be ______Mr2.
solution:
IA=Mr22+M7r−r22+M(6r)212+M3r−r22

=Mr22+M(169)r24+3Mr2+25Mr24=171Mr24+37Mr24=2084Mr2=52Mr2

subject: Physics
q: Moment of inertia about an axis $AB$ for a rod of mass $40$ kg and length $3$ m is same as that of a solid sphere of mass of $10$ kg and radius $R$ about an axis parallel to $AB$ axis with separation of $3$ m as shown in figure below. The value of $R$ is given as $\sqrt{\dfrac{\alpha}{2}}$. The value of $\alpha$ is _______.
question: Moment of inertia about an axis $AB$ for a rod of mass $40$ kg and length $3$ m is same as that of a solid sphere of mass of $10$ kg and radius $R$ about an axis parallel to $AB$ axis with separation of $3$ m as shown in figure below. The value of $R$ is given as $\sqrt{\dfrac{\alpha}{2}}$. The value of $\alpha$ is _______.
solution:
Moment of inertia of the rod about axis $AB$ passing through one of its ends:
$I_{\text{rod}} = \dfrac{ML^2}{3} = \dfrac{40 \times (3)^2}{3} = \dfrac{40 \times 9}{3} = 120\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2$

Moment of inertia of the solid sphere about the same axis $AB$, using the parallel axis theorem $I = I_{\text{cm}} + md^2$:
Mass $m = 10\text{ kg}$, radius $R$, distance from the centre of the sphere to axis $AB$ is $d = 3\text{ m}$.

$I_{\text{sphere}} = \dfrac{2}{5}mR^2 + md^2 = \dfrac{2}{5}(10)R^2 + 10(3)^2 = 4R^2 + 90$

Equating the two moments of inertia:
$120 = 4R^2 + 90$
$4R^2 = 30$
$R^2 = \dfrac{15}{2}$
$R = \sqrt{\dfrac{15}{2}}$

Comparing with $R = \sqrt{\dfrac{\alpha}{2}}$:
$\alpha = 15$

Hence, $\alpha = 15$.

subject: Physics
q: Suppose there is a uniform circular disc of mass $M \mathrm{~kg}$ and radius $r \mathrm{~m}$ shown in figure. The shaded regions are cut out from the disc. The moment of inertia of the remainder about the axis $A$ of the disc is given by $\frac{x}{256} M r^{2}$. The value of $x$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
question: Suppose there is a uniform circular disc of mass $M \mathrm{~kg}$ and radius $r \mathrm{~m}$ shown in figure. The shaded regions are cut out from the disc. The moment of inertia of the remainder about the axis $A$ of the disc is given by $\frac{x}{256} M r^{2}$. The value of $x$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
The moment of inertia of the complete disc about axis A is
$I_{disc} = \frac{1}{2}Mr^2 = \frac{128Mr^2}{256}$.
Each removed circular region has radius $r/4$ and mass $m_i = M/(r^2/16r^2) = M/16$.
For each removed disc at distance $3r/4$ from axis A, using the parallel axis theorem:
$I_{removed} = \frac{1}{2}m_i(r/4)^2 + m_i(3r/4)^2 = \frac{Mr^2}{512} + \frac{9Mr^2}{256} = \frac{19Mr^2}{512}$.
For two removed discs:
$I_{2removed} = \frac{38Mr^2}{512} = \frac{19Mr^2}{256}$.
Therefore:
$I_{remainder} = \frac{128Mr^2}{256} - \frac{19Mr^2}{256} = \frac{109Mr^2}{256}$, so $x = 109$.

q: A circular disc reaches from top to bottom of an inclined plane of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mo>.</mo></math> When it slips down the plane, it takes time  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. When it rolls down the plane, it takes time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> will be
question: A circular disc reaches from top to bottom of an inclined plane of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi><mo>.</mo></math> When it slips down the plane, it takes time  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>. When it rolls down the plane, it takes time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mfrac></msqrt></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> will be
solution:
If disk slips on inclined plane, then its acceleration, a1=gsinθ
L=12a1t12
⇒t1=2 La1 …i

If disk rolls on inclined plane, its acceleration,
a2=gsinθ1+ImR2
a2=gsinθ1+mR22 mR2
a2=23gsinθ
Now L=12a2·t22
⇒t2=2 La2 …ii

Now, t2t1=a1a2=32
⇒x=2

subject: Physics
q:
This question has Statement 1 and Statement 2. Of the four choices given after the Statements, choose the one that best describes the two Statements.
Statement 1: When moment of inertia $I$ of a body rotating about an axis with angular speed $\omega$ increases, its angular momentum $L$ is unchanged but the kinetic energy $K$ increases if there is no torque applied on it.
Statement 2: $L=I \omega$, kinetic energy of rotation $=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2$
question:
This question has Statement 1 and Statement 2. Of the four choices given after the Statements, choose the one that best describes the two Statements.
Statement 1: When moment of inertia $I$ of a body rotating about an axis with angular speed $\omega$ increases, its angular momentum $L$ is unchanged but the kinetic energy $K$ increases if there is no torque applied on it.
Statement 2: $L=I \omega$, kinetic energy of rotation $=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2$
options: Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is false, Statement 2 is true.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is correct explanation of the Statement 1.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is false.
solution:
As $L=I \omega$ so $L$ increases with increase in $\omega$. Kinetic energy $_{\text {(rotational) }}$ depends on an angular velocity ' $\omega$ ' and moment of inertia of the body $I$.
i.e., $K \cdot E \cdot\left(\right.$ rotational $=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2$

subject: Physics
q: Two small balls with masses $m$ and 2 m are attached to both ends of a rigid rod of length $d$ and negligible mass. If angular momentum of this system is $L$ about an axis ($A$) passing through its centre of mass and perpendicular to the rod then angular velocity of the system about $A$ is :
question: Two small balls with masses $m$ and 2 m are attached to both ends of a rigid rod of length $d$ and negligible mass. If angular momentum of this system is $L$ about an axis ($A$) passing through its centre of mass and perpendicular to the rod then angular velocity of the system about $A$ is :
solution:
CM from mass $m$: $x_{cm} = \frac{2md}{3m} = \frac{2d}{3}$.
Distances: $m$ is $2d/3$ from CM, $2m$ is $d/3$ from CM.
$I_{cm} = m(2d/3)^2 + 2m(d/3)^2 = \frac{4md^2}{9} + \frac{2md^2}{9} = \frac{2md^2}{3}$.
$L = I\omega \Rightarrow \omega = \frac{L}{I} = \frac{3L}{2md^2}$.

The densities of materials are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>5</mn></math>. The moment of inertia of these discs respectively about their diameters will be in the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is ______.
question: Two discs of same mass and different radii are made of different materials such that their thicknesses are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> respectively. The densities of materials are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>5</mn></math>. The moment of inertia of these discs respectively about their diameters will be in the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is ______.
solution:
Mass of the disc in terms of density can be written as, m=ρπR2t.

⇒R2=mρπt

Now moment of inertia of the disc about the diameter is, mR24.

Therefore,

I=mR24=m24ρπt⇒I∝1ρt

Required ratio, I1I2=ρ2t2ρ1t1=53×0.51=56

Hence, x=5.

question:

[The coefficient of static friction, μs, is 0.4 ]
solution:
Let's take solid cylinder is in equilibrium

T+f=mgsin60

TR-fR=0

Solving we get

T=freq=mgsinθ2

But limiting friction < required friction

μmgcos60°<mgsin60°2

∴ Hence cylinder will not remain in equilibrium

Hence f= kinetic

=μkN

=μkmgcos60°

=mg5

q: A heavy iron bar of weight <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a man. The rod makes an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal, the normal force applied by the man on bar is:
question: A heavy iron bar of weight <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a man. The rod makes an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal, the normal force applied by the man on bar is:
solution:
Let's consider the following diagram:

For the equilibrium of the system, the net torque, according to the above figure, on the system about point A should be zero.

With reference to the above figure, the net anti-clockwise torque about A is

τa=F2L   ...1

And, the net clockwise torque about the same point is

τc=Wcosθ×L2    ...2

Equations (1) and (2) imply that

Wcosθ×L2=F2L⇒12×10×cos60°×L2=F2 L⇒F2=120×10×cos60°2=30

Hence, the required weight can be expressed as

F2=3 kg-wt

subject: Physics
q: Two masses 400 g and 350 g are suspended from the ends of a light string passing over a heavy pulley of radius 2 cm. When released from rest the heavier mass is observed to fall 81 cm in 9 s. The rotational inertia of the pulley is $\_\_\_\_$ $\mathrm{kg} \cdot \mathrm{m}^{2}$. $\left(\mathrm{g}=9.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)$
question: Two masses 400 g and 350 g are suspended from the ends of a light string passing over a heavy pulley of radius 2 cm. When released from rest the heavier mass is observed to fall 81 cm in 9 s. The rotational inertia of the pulley is $\_\_\_\_$ $\mathrm{kg} \cdot \mathrm{m}^{2}$. $\left(\mathrm{g}=9.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)$
solution:
From kinematics with $s = 0.81$ m and $t = 9$ s: $a = 2s/t^2 = 0.02$ m/s².
For the pulley system, applying Newton's second law to both masses and torque equation: $(m_1 - m_2)g - (m_1 + m_2)a = Ia/R^2$.
Substituting values: $0.05 \times 9.8 - 0.75 \times 0.02 = I \times 50$ gives $0.49 - 0.015 = 50I$.
Therefore $I = 9.5 \times 10^{-3}$ kg·m²

subject: Physics
q: A circular disc $X$ of radius $R$ is made from an iron plate of thickness $t$, and another disc $Y$ of radius $4 \mathrm{R}$ is made from an iron plate of thickness $\frac{\mathrm{t}}{4}$. Then the relation between the moment of inertia $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{X}}$ and $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Y}}$ is
question: A circular disc $X$ of radius $R$ is made from an iron plate of thickness $t$, and another disc $Y$ of radius $4 \mathrm{R}$ is made from an iron plate of thickness $\frac{\mathrm{t}}{4}$. Then the relation between the moment of inertia $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{X}}$ and $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Y}}$ is
solution:
$$
\mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mR} \quad \text { or } \quad \mathrm{M} \propto \mathrm{t} \propto \mathrm{R}^2
$$
For $\operatorname{disc} X, I_x=\frac{1}{2}(m)(R)^2=\frac{1}{2}\left(\pi r^2 t\right) .(R)^2$
For disc $Y, I_y=\frac{1}{2}\left[\pi(4 R)^2 \cdot t / 4\right][4 R]^2$
$$
\Rightarrow \frac{I_x}{I_y}=\frac{1}{(4)^3} \Rightarrow I_y=64 I_x
$$

Moment of inertia of a hollow cylinder whose inner radius id r and outer radius is R is given as

m202+1022=mk2

q:

  List-I   List-II
(A) Moment of inertia of solid sphere
of radius R about any tangent.		 (I) 53MR2
(B) Moment of inertia of hollow sphere of radius
R about any tangent.		 (II) 75MR2
(C) Moment of inertia of circular ring of radius
R about its diameter.		 (III) 14MR2

question:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
(A) Moment of inertia of solid sphere
of radius R about any tangent.		 (I) 53MR2
(B) Moment of inertia of hollow sphere of radius
R about any tangent.		 (II) 75MR2
(C) Moment of inertia of circular ring of radius
R about its diameter.		 (III) 14MR2
(D) Moment of inertia of circular disc of radius
R about any diameter.		 (IV) 12MR2
solution:
For solid sphere

I0=Icom +MR2  (Using parallel axis theorem)

I0=25MR2+MR2

I0=75MR2⇒A→II

For hollow sphere

I=Icom+MR2⇒I=53MR2⇒B→I

For Circular ring

I1+I2=I3 ( Using perpendicular axis theorem)

By symmetry MOI

About 1 and 2 Axis are same i.e.

I1=I2

∴  2I1=I3=MR2   ∵Icom=MR2

I1=MR22⇒ C→IV

Similarly in disc

2I1=MR22∵Icom=MR22

I1=MR24⇒D→III

q: A rod of linear mass density ' $\lambda$ ' and length ' $L$ ' is bent to form a ring of radius 'R'. Moment of inertia of ring about any of its diameter is :
question: A rod of linear mass density ' $\lambda$ ' and length ' $L$ ' is bent to form a ring of radius 'R'. Moment of inertia of ring about any of its diameter is :
solution:
$L=2 \pi R$
$\mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{2}=\frac{\lambda \times \mathrm{L}}{2} \times\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2 \pi}\right)^2=\frac{\lambda \mathrm{L}^3}{8 \pi^2}$

subject: Physics
q: A uniform circular disc of radius ' R ' and mass ' M ' is rotating about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through its centre. A small circular part of radius $\mathrm{R} / 2$ is removed from the original disc as shown in the figure. Find the moment of inertia of the remaining part of the original disc about the axis as given above.
question: A uniform circular disc of radius ' R ' and mass ' M ' is rotating about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through its centre. A small circular part of radius $\mathrm{R} / 2$ is removed from the original disc as shown in the figure. Find the moment of inertia of the remaining part of the original disc about the axis as given above.
solution: $\begin{aligned} & I_{\text {complete }}=\frac{M R^2}{2} \\ & \begin{aligned} I_{\text {removed }} & =\left(\frac{M}{4}\right)\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2 \frac{1}{2}+\left(\frac{M}{4}\right)\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{3 M R^2}{32} \\ I=\frac{M R^2}{2} & -\frac{3 M R^2}{32}=\frac{13}{32} M R^2\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: One solid sphere A and another hollow sphere B are of same mass and same outer radii. Their moment of inertia about their diameters are respectively $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{A}}$ and $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{B}}$ such that
question: One solid sphere A and another hollow sphere B are of same mass and same outer radii. Their moment of inertia about their diameters are respectively $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{A}}$ and $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{B}}$ such that
options:
yes, $45^{\circ}$
$I_A / I_B=d_A / d_B$
Where $d_A$ and $d_B$ are their densities.
solution:
Moment of inertia of a uniform density solid sphere, $A=\frac{2}{5} M R^2$
Since $M$ and $R$ are same, $I_A < I_B$.

subject: Physics
q: Four particles, each of mass 1 kg are placed at four corners of a square of side 2 m. The moment of inertia of the system about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through one of its vertex is ______ kg m2.
question: Four particles, each of mass 1 kg are placed at four corners of a square of side 2 m. The moment of inertia of the system about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through one of its vertex is ______ kg m2.
solution:
The formula to calculate the moment of inertia of a system of particles about a point can be written as

I=∑i=1nmiri2   ...1

where, mi is the mass of the ith particle situated at a distance ri from the point.

With reference to the above diagram, it can be written that the net moment of inertia of the system of particles is

I=ma2+ma2+m(2a)2=4ma2=4×1 kg×2 m2=16 kg m2

The ratio of their radius of gyrations respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>c</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>l</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is _______.
question: A solid sphere and a solid cylinder of same mass and radius are rolling on a horizontal surface without slipping. The ratio of their radius of gyrations respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>c</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>l</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is _______.
solution:
Considering rotational axis as the diametrical axis for sphere and axis of cylinder. Then

K12=25R2 and K22=12R2

∴ K1K2=2/51/2=45

⇒K1K2=25

Comparing it with the given value in the question,

25=2x

∴ x=5

subject: Physics
q: Two iron solid discs of negligible thickness have radii $R_1$ and $R_2$ and moment of intertia $I_1$ and $I_2$, respectively. For $R_2=2 R_1$, the ratio of $I_1$ and $I_2$ would be $1 / x$, where $\mathrm{x}=$ ________ -
question: Two iron solid discs of negligible thickness have radii $R_1$ and $R_2$ and moment of intertia $I_1$ and $I_2$, respectively. For $R_2=2 R_1$, the ratio of $I_1$ and $I_2$ would be $1 / x$, where $\mathrm{x}=$ ________ -
solution: Given $R_2=2 R_1$ $\begin{aligned} & M_1=\sigma \times \pi R_1^2=M_o \\ & M_2=\sigma \times \pi R_2^2=M_o \\ & M_2=\sigma \times \pi R_2^2=\sigma \times \pi\left[2 R_1\right]^2=\sigma \times 4 \pi R_1^2=4 M_o \end{aligned}$ $\frac{I_1}{I_2}=\frac{\frac{M_1 R_1^2}{2}}{\frac{M_2 R_2^2}{2}}=\frac{M_1 R_1^2}{M_2 R_2^2}=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{4}=\frac{1}{16}$

subject: Physics
q: Two cars $A$ and $B$ each of mass $10^{3} \mathrm{~kg}$ are moving on parallel tracks separated by a distance of 10 m, in same direction with speeds $72 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ and $36 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$. The magnitude of angular momentum of $\operatorname{car} A$ with respect to $\operatorname{car} B$ is $\_\_\_\_$ J.s.
question: Two cars $A$ and $B$ each of mass $10^{3} \mathrm{~kg}$ are moving on parallel tracks separated by a distance of 10 m, in same direction with speeds $72 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$ and $36 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}$. The magnitude of angular momentum of $\operatorname{car} A$ with respect to $\operatorname{car} B$ is $\_\_\_\_$ J.s.
solution:
Mass of each car $m = 10^3$ kg.
Speed of car $A$, $v_A = 72$ km/h $= 72 \times \frac{5}{18} = 20$ m/s.
Speed of car $B$, $v_B = 36$ km/h $= 36 \times \frac{5}{18} = 10$ m/s.
Relative velocity of car $A$ with respect to car $B$ is $v_{AB} = v_A - v_B = 20 - 10 = 10$ m/s.
The cars are moving on parallel tracks separated by a perpendicular distance $d = 10$ m.
The magnitude of angular momentum of car $A$ with respect to car $B$ is given by $L = m \times v_{AB} \times d$.
Substituting the values: $L = 10^3 \times 10 \times 10 = 10^5$ J.s.
Thus, the correct option is (2).

question: Two identical spherical balls of mass M and radius R each are stuck on two ends of a rod of length 2R and mass M(see figure). The moment of inertia of the system about the axis passing perpendicularly through the centre of the rod is
solution:
Applying parallel axis theorem for the balls,
Ib=25MR2+M2R2


Itotal=2Ib+IR
Itotal=25MR2+M2R2×2+M122R2

Itotal=13715MR2
